questions.q: Parallel lines form corresponding angles of $76^{\circ}$ and $x$. Find $x$.

options.why: Treats corresponding angles as supplementary instead of equal.
Halves the known angle instead of matching its measure.
Subtracts from a full turn instead of using equal angles.

questions.q: Parallel lines form alternate angles of $121^{\circ}$ and $x$. Find $x$.

options.why: Treats alternate angles as supplementary instead of equal.
Subtracts from $190$ rather than matching the equal angle.
Subtracts from a full turn instead of using equal angles.

questions.q: Parallel lines form co-interior angles of $109^{\circ}$ and $x$. Find $x$.

options.why: Treats co-interior angles as equal instead of supplementary.
Subtracts from $360$ degrees instead of $180$ degrees.
Subtracts from $128$ rather than the supplementary total.

questions.q: When may corresponding-angle equality be used?

options.text: When the crossed lines are known to be parallel
Whenever any two lines are crossed
options.why: Ignores the required condition that the crossed lines are parallel.

options.text: Only when the transversal is perpendicular
options.why: Adds an unnecessary right-angle condition to the property.

options.text: Only when both angles are acute
options.why: Adds an angle-size condition that the property does not require.
